old_claim.note: Communications Satellites provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct communications-satellite dependency remains because the scoped Vela constellation used coordinated detection satellites for worldwide nuclear-test monitoring, demonstrating the architecture outside the communications application class.

source_shape.source_locator: Primary Science section: states that each satellite in a Vela pair monitored one half of Earth for nuclear explosions and describes the mission's gamma-ray detection instruments.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: NASA documents coordinated global coverage by paired nuclear-detection satellites, providing a non-communications implementation of constellation architecture.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Communications is one payload and service category among many; complementary coverage by detection spacecraft proves that category is not causal infrastructure for constellations in general.

ontology_before: The general constellation class depended on communications satellites, conflating one application domain with the multi-spacecraft architecture.
ontology_after: Satellite constellations are defined by coordinated orbital coverage and can support detection, navigation, communications, or other missions.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: communications_satellites is absent as a direct prerequisite of satellite_constellations.
would_reject_if: The node is narrowed specifically to communications constellations rather than coordinated satellite systems generally.
The Vela spacecraft are shown to have been communications satellites rather than nuclear-detection satellites.
short_reason: Vela demonstrated coordinated constellation coverage without being a communications-satellite system.
